name: KrillApp.DataPoint.Filter.DiscardBelow
title: Discard Below
description: Rejects DataPoint snapshots where the value falls below a configured minimum threshold, stored in this filter's own meta.snapshot.value as a stringified number. When a snapshot value is less than the threshold, it is discarded and not stored. Prevents outliers, sensor errors, or invalid low readings from entering the data store. The filter only takes effect when the owning DataPoint lists it in its meta.inputs — parenting alone does nothing.
shortDescription: Discards snapshots with values below a configured minimum threshold.
llmPurpose: Rejects incoming DataPoint snapshots where the value falls below a configured minimum threshold.
llmBehavior: evaluates snapshot value < threshold from its own meta.snapshot.value
discards snapshot if condition is true
llmInputs: minimum threshold stored in this filter's meta.snapshot.value (stringified number)
llmTypicalUseCases: filter out zero or below-zero values
llmCreationHints: Create a DiscardBelow filter when the user wants to reject values below a minimum. Store the threshold as a stringified number in the filter's meta.snapshot.value (FilterMetaData has no value field), and list the filter in the owning DataPoint's meta.inputs — a filter merely parented under a DataPoint does nothing.

llmConnectionHints.role: leaf filter node that gates incoming snapshot values
llmConnectionHints.wiring: Passive: this filter uses no sources or invocationTriggers; the owning DataPoint must list it in its meta.inputs for it to be evaluated during ingest, and the threshold lives in the filter's own meta.snapshot.value.
